episode_summary: A Little Fish bonus episode where the team shares listener‑submitted mini-facts from the past week. Highlights include Stonehenge vs the A303, family pairings in sport, Canada’s “storm chips,” and odd news like a fatal falling poodle and runaway hamsters grounding a plane.

facts.fact: The A303 was not built close to Stonehenge. Stonehenge was built close to the A303.
So Swindon Wildcats are a hockey team, a nice hockey team. And this year, not one, but two father and son duos played on the same roster.
Canadians have special crisps to eat during storms.
On October 24, 1988, a falling poodle calls the death of three people in Buenos Aires.
